title: Charred Korean Vegetable Shortening Mandu
description: A charred, shaggy, crowd-pleaser Korean dish featuring vegetable shortening, double-fry-KFC style, nut-free-friendly, in a delicious mandu form.
instructions: Combine flour, cornstarch, and salt in a bowl to make the dough.
Gradually add vegetable shortening to the dough and knead until smooth.
Rest the dough for 30 minutes to allow the gluten to relax.
Preheat a deep frying pan with about 2-3 inches of vegetable oil to 350°F.
Roll out the dough to about 1/4 inch thickness and cut into desired mandu shapes.
Double-fry the mandu in batches until golden brown, then set aside.
In a separate pan, heat some oil and cook the scallions, garlic, and ginger until fragrant.
Add the soy sauce, chili flakes, and sesame seeds to the pan and stir-fry for a few seconds.
Add the cooked mandu to the pan and toss to coat with the sauce.
Serve hot and charred, garnished with additional scallions and sesame seeds if desired.
